transcript.text: Hi, sorry I'm late, just got off another call.
Hello.
All right, perfect.
So I think Eric's also going to join us, but just to kind of do a quick recap.

So Bailey and I have been kind of brainstorming like the content calendar moving forward and like what are types of topics that we want to present to you, considering like your conversation with Eric on Friday and also like the suggestions that you shared in the Airtable that you put together.
So Bailey, if you want to do like a quick breakdown of like the keyword research that you've done and like the assignments that you think we want to do.
Yeah, so I haven't put it all over into Airtable yet because I'm still sort of in progress.
But basically what I did is I took the direction that you talked about and on the Friday.
Bailey, Thank you.
Anything that I missed and some of what we've been talking about, which was targeting underwriting workbenches and also sort of more the underwriter persona.

So some of it was taking the existing keywords and just kind of changing the angle that we were going at it from, making it more specific to things like tools, making it more specific to the personas.

And I also took the content ideas that you had dropped in your own Airtable and sort of went through those and thought more about how we could map kind of the topics to the keywords to make sure that we were still kind of optimizing for things that were actually going to match search intent as well.

And so right now I'm feeding those into our assignment generator and today I should be able to share, I'm targeting an additional like 30 topics or so in our content OS Airtable so you can take a look at what the actual article.

And one of the other things I did was based on, I watched the video of your conversation with Eric, was sort of look at the different components of underwriting workbenches and see if we could kind of break that apart.
Because you talked about how it really started out as a CRM and sort of there was like the evolution of it and maybe use some of that to capture search intent too.
So people who might be searching for more like point specific tools, but we could create content that funnels them to more of a integrated platform as well.
Yeah.
And not, I don't, I wouldn't say CRM or case management.
That's just the analogy that I'm giving to you to represent sort of a legacy software category.
So probably you could do some deep research to understand kind of, and the report I sent you would talk about sort of what's in, what's out.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay.
I think that the point is the majority of the category is people coming from.
The underwriter, UI, workbench type of angle.
And then, you know, we come from the more decision intelligence angle.
Everyone claims to do everything in the world of AI, probably decision intelligence.
So at least that's what the analysts are telling us is actually more valuable.
So we want to surround that.
Yeah.
Sorry, I didn't mean to interrupt you.
There are certain, like, terms, like, you know, decision intelligence and insurance that, like, don't have, like, a zero volume.
But I think there's other ways into the topic.
Like, there's a lot of people searching for business intelligence and tools.
And there's some overlap there, too, I think, that we can talk about.
Some of those we can just do it as it's great for, you know, even if it's low volume for sort of building, you know, having that content exist.
And it's coming at it from your angle and it feels like more thought leadership.
And then I think some of it we can come at from in terms of search volume from different.
Okay.
Yeah.
So I'm looking at the ones that we've already done.
I don't think any of those are wasted.
It was more concern of like the backlog.
I was like, oh, there's not, there's not enough in the backlog.
Um, so I think this is a good time to, to go through this exercise.
The backlog is, um, starts to take us in some other directions.
So if we think this is a media enough, um, area to occupy for, you know, at least a few months, so then we can, you know, move on to other clusters after that.
Um, that was my, I was just struggling to find, uh, things in the backlog that were, uh, as much on point.
Um, and so what I'll do today, I should be able to add, um, another 30 or so topics.
Okay.
Into, uh, maybe I'll, I'll add another label so they don't get all mixed together.
Um, and then you can take a look at those, see if those feel like a better fit for the direction we're going.
Bye-bye.
And we can kind of use that as our jumping off point, too, because I don't want to put 100 in there and, you know, that's a lot to wade through.
So I'll add another 25 to 30 based on these conversations, and you can let us know based on those as well.
Okay.
And the article ideas that I generated, to what degree are those useful?
Because a little bit of it is like AI just doing its thing, but I think some of it has like angles that are representative of not heavy thought leadership, but medium-sized thought leadership.
Yeah.
So that's actually what Bailey and I were discussing earlier.
So there are some ideas here that we can use to just cite as a springboard for keyword research, like, for example, future of underwriting.
This one has like a relatively low search volume, but it's like future of insurance under.
So it's right on point with this topic idea, also underwriting intelligence, that's another keyword that exists, like underwriting alpha, that's probably something that also exists in SEMrush.
So we can use those as like jumping off points.
There are some ideas here that are more aligned with like pure thought leadership, for example, like the hidden cost of making underwriters just copy and paste.

This is like a great idea for like a thought leadership blog post that you could like distribute in your newsletter or like on LinkedIn, but it would require, this would just not be an SEO blog, but what we can use with like this short description here, for example, we can actually use this idea, just like we can add it to SEO blog posts that talk about, you know, like underwriters and like their like day-to-day tasks.
So like different tools, and we could like add this, a mention of like what the hidden cost is of making underwriters like copy and paste things constantly.
So we can still use these things, but this like is more thought leadership and like the true sense of the word.
Yeah.
Okay.
I got, I got, you know, the team is always having meetings and coming up with angles.
So I would like to figure out a way to feed those into this pipeline.
That's not, that's not one that's like an important angle, but there's probably some other ones in here that are relevant.
So like one came up, you know, recently.
So I don't know, how would I send you those notes?
What's the best way to send you the notes from recent sales calls or expert interviews?
I think.
So we have in our Notion base, let me just find it really quickly.
Drop them in there and then just tag Bailey.
Yeah, yeah, that works.
If you go to client docs, files and shared context, you can just drop them here and yeah.
Okay, because we're looking to do a weekly or bi-weekly AI post, maybe it's more thought leadership, but hopefully it can serve both purposes.
So as we get interview notes for that, I'd like to include them into this pipeline so it's one of the five or one of the ten for that period.
It'll just save us an extra step of having to like, you know, basically the post comes with a little outline, but then we could feed it into your ideation pipeline.
Okay, so you mean like using Atlas to generate blog posts from outlines that you provide to us?
Yeah, yeah, like maybe once every other week.
Okay, yeah, I think that...
You could like rip up the outline.
It's just about, you know, there's something in there that's somewhat original.
And so then the article comes off as not just like total rehash, but it starts with something that's a little bit original at its core.
Yeah, yeah, totally.
No, I think we can...
mean, once we get the authentic pipeline set up, we can start feeding those in and then evaluate the output and see like if we might need to like customize it further.
But yeah, we can definitely do that.
I actually done something similar for a different client when I just joined GrowthX.
So they were doing a lot of SEO content and then they wanted to pivot and go very like...
tool...
to is the solution and

It really specific content around topics that was very close to their what like what their service was and they provided us with like for every cluster that we were doing they provided us with like a document on what their point of view was on this specific topic like what are the tools that we should mention like different angles and then we would use that to generate content and it was a lot more opinionated than like the standards.
SEO blog.
and it, yeah, they perform pretty well as well.
Yeah, that's what I'd like to figure out since it's so complicated some of these topics.
think surrounding a known category is a little less complicated, but as we go adjacencies, it gets more complicated.
So how do we feed you expert interviews?
That's what I was asking, Eric, you know, do we, do you have a set of questions that would be provocative questions or maybe just every two weeks?
Should I just send you a call recording with someone?
You know, what's the best format to have?
Maybe I should just create the notes, basically, the more explicit notes.
Yeah, I think there are two ways.
I mean, it really depends on the topics that we decide to target.
So I think once Bailey presents the content assignments, we're going to be able to see if we can just do one or two interviews with an in-house expert.
And then we can use those insights for, like, multiple blogs or if there are some topics that are going to need, like, more tailored input.
Yeah.
But I've done this.
I mean, I've done for previous clients.
I have interviewed, like, subject matter experts at their company and used a transcript to provide, like, quotes and, like, original insights for articles.
So, I mean, we have experience with, like, both.
using online like this.
But
So, you know, I'm happy to interview and, you know, no, I mean, that's not your model, but I want to just give you more context.
Like, I don't think that you have to do the interview.
I just think if you just say, you know, here's 10 articles and then give it to someone and they can just record some some perspectives on those 10 topics.

So once you come back with that, that, that list, I'll sort of, let's see, I can just go through, approve, you know, five or 10 of them, but then also go out to a expert to start gathering some more context for those.
Yeah, yeah, that sounds good.
All right, good.
I'll look forward to that.
So when should I look out for that?
I should be able to send it today.
Okay.
All right.
Great.
Because I, I know we're trying to find some articles for this week, right?
Yes, exactly.
I will, I'll watch out.
Yeah, I'll watch out for your message and try to come back to you quickly.
Thanks.
All right.
I'll talk to you on Friday.
Sure.
Thank you.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss content strategy and keyword research for underwriting workbench topics, aligning with recent conversations and suggestions.](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=8.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Bailey conducted keyword research focusing on underwriting workbenches and underwriter personas, aiming to add \~30 new topics to the content calendar](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=38.0)
  - [David emphasized the importance of decision intelligence angle and thought leadership content, suggesting a weekly/bi-weekly AI post](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=504.0)
  - [Team agreed to incorporate expert insights and interview notes into the content creation process for more original, tailored content](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=441.0)

## Topics

### Keyword Research and Content Strategy

  - [Bailey conducted keyword research based on underwriting workbenches and underwriter personas](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=38.0)
  - [Shifted focus from CRM/case management to decision intelligence and underwriter UI](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)
  - [Targeting an additional 30 topics to be added to the content OS Airtable](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=103.0)
  - [Exploring components of underwriting workbenches to capture search intent for integrated platforms](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Balancing low-volume, thought leadership keywords (e.g., "decision intelligence in insurance") with higher-volume related terms (e.g., "business intelligence tools")](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)

### Thought Leadership and Expert Input

  - [David provided article ideas mixing AI-generated content with medium-sized thought leadership angles](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=317.0)
  - [Team to incorporate sales call notes and expert interviews into the content pipeline](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=441.0)
  - [Agreed on creating a weekly/bi-weekly AI post with more original, thought leadership content](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=504.0)
  - [Aida suggested using Atlas to generate blog posts from outlines provided by David's team](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=504.0)

### Content Creation Process

  - [Bailey to add 25-30 new topics to Airtable with a new label for easy identification](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)
  - [David to review and approve 5-10 topics from the new list](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)
  - [Team to explore interviewing in-house experts or using recorded perspectives on approved topics](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=667.0)
  - [Aida shared experience in interviewing subject matter experts and using transcripts for original insights](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=667.0)

### SEO and Thought Leadership Balance

  - [Some topics (e.g., "future of underwriting") have low search volume but align well with thought leadership](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=339.0)
  - [Team to incorporate thought leadership ideas into SEO-focused content where possible](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
  - [Exploring ways to create more opinionated, company-specific content while maintaining SEO value](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=587.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Bailey to send \~30 new topic ideas to David today for review](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)
  - [David to quickly review and approve topics to meet this week's content needs](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=761.0)
  - [David to provide expert insights or interview notes for approved topics](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=647.0)
  - [Team to set up a process for incorporating expert input into content creation](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=441.0)
  - [Explore creating a weekly/bi-weekly AI post with more original content](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=504.0)
  - [Next check-in scheduled for Friday](https://fathom.video/share/uatYF4vj-8tNs9wXzmuPRPNAUcrAGvCU?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)

action_items.description: Add 25-30 new content topics to Airtable w/ new label. Focus on underwriting workbenches, underwriter persona, decision intelligence. Include keyword research + assignment generator output.
Add recent sales call/expert interview notes to Notion (Client docs > Files and shared context). Tag Bailey.
Review new content topics in Airtable when notified. Approve 5-10 topics for expert input gathering.
